title: MCP Tool Reference
tagline: Every mcp__dbt_index__ tool, signatures and returns
summary: Complete reference for all sixteen mcp__dbt_index__* tools dbt Wizard exposes. Every call is a structured MCP tool call backed by a local DuckDB index of the dbt project (models, columns, tests, edges) plus read-only warehouse access, never a file read or grep. Tools fall into eight groups: DAG-aware intelligence, the project index, warehouse integration, dbt-native CLI calls, business context, MetricFlow metrics, build timings, and dev-vs-prod diff. dbt CLI tools auto-refresh the index on success and support deferred state for cost-efficient builds.
whyDifferent: A general AI coding agent answers questions by reading .sql and .yml files and grepping for strings, so "what breaks if I drop brand_name" becomes a text match against files that happen to mention the column. These tools query a live DuckDB index that already knows grain, primary keys, materializations, test pass/fail state, and DAG edges, then resolve {{ ref() }} and {{ source() }} against the warehouse. The result is column-level blast radius with severity ratings and live data previews instead of a list of files that contain a substring.

capabilities.title: DAG-aware intelligence
capabilities.detail: impact, lineage, and timings traverse the actual edge graph. impact returns severity-rated column- and node-level blast radius; lineage does depth-controlled upstream/downstream traversal scoped to a subgraph; timings finds critical-path bottlenecks from real run results and OpenTelemetry traces.

capabilities.title: Live project index
capabilities.detail: describe, search, and changeset read the local DuckDB index. describe gives full node introspection (columns, compiled SQL, tests, lineage, catalog types, grain, PK markers); search does BM25 metadata or regex-against-SQL with DAG context attached; changeset does epoch-based diff since last flush.

capabilities.title: Warehouse integration
capabilities.detail: warehouse runs read-only SQL directly against Snowflake/BigQuery/etc for live previews with no dbt run; dbt_show resolves full Jinja ({{ ref() }}, {{ source() }}, macros) and executes against the warehouse.

capabilities.title: dbt-native CLI calls
capabilities.detail: dbt_build, dbt_run, dbt_compile, dbt_test, and dbt_parse are structured tool calls (not shell strings). They auto-refresh the index on success and support --defer --state for cost-efficient deferred dev builds.

capabilities.title: Business context
capabilities.detail: context searches glossary/fiscal/org docs with synonym resolution, mapping business jargon like "annual revenue" to canonical dbt terms (ARR) and the models that implement them.

capabilities.title: Metrics, timings, and diff
capabilities.detail: metrics queries the MetricFlow semantic layer (list/describe/run/dry-run); timings exposes slowest nodes and critical path; diff compares local vs production state (via dbt Cloud token or deferred state) for safe PR validation.

examplePrompts.prompt: What breaks if I drop brand_name from stg_products?
examplePrompts.intent: Column-level blast radius before a destructive change

examplePrompts.prompt: Show me everything two hops downstream of fct_orders.
examplePrompts.intent: Scoped downstream lineage traversal with depth control

examplePrompts.prompt: What's the grain and PK of int_orders_enriched, and are its tests passing?
examplePrompts.intent: Full node introspection from the index

examplePrompts.prompt: Find every model that references the discount_pct column.
Preview the top 10 rows of dim_customers in the warehouse without running dbt.
Build int_orders_enriched and everything downstream using deferred prod state, then run its tests.
examplePrompts.intent: Cost-efficient deferred dev build plus tests

examplePrompts.prompt: What does our glossary mean by annual revenue and which model owns it, and what's the slowest node in the build?

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__impact
tools.purpose: DAG-aware blast radius for a node or specific column, with severity ratings, by traversing edges in the index rather than grepping references.

params.name: node
params.desc: Target model/node, e.g. stg_products.

params.name: column
params.desc: Optional column to scope blast radius to a single field, e.g. brand_name.

params.name: max_depth
params.desc: Optional downstream hop limit for the traversal.

params.name: scope
params.desc: Optional subgraph filter (e.g. layer or path) to bound the report.

tools.returns: Ranked list of affected downstream nodes/columns each tagged severity critical/high/medium/low, with the edge path explaining why.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__lineage
tools.purpose: Upstream/downstream DAG traversal with hop-depth control and edge-type filtering, scoped to a subgraph.

params.name: node
params.desc: Starting node, e.g. fct_orders.

params.name: direction
params.desc: upstream, downstream, or both.

params.name: max_depth
params.desc: Number of hops to traverse.

params.name: edge_types
params.desc: Filter edges (e.g. ref vs source) included in traversal.

params.name: scope
params.desc: Optional subgraph bound.

tools.returns: Ordered node list with hop distance and edge type per relationship.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__describe
tools.purpose: Full node introspection from the index: columns, compiled SQL, test results, lineage, catalog types, plus grain, PK markers, schema, and materialization.

params.name: node
params.desc: Node to introspect, e.g. int_orders_enriched.

params.name: include
params.desc: Optional sections to return (columns, compiled_sql, tests, lineage, catalog).

tools.returns: Structured node profile: column list with types, grain, PK markers, materialization, schema, last-run test pass/fail, and compiled SQL.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__search
tools.purpose: BM25 metadata search or regex against raw/compiled SQL, with DAG context attached to every hit.

params.name: query
params.desc: Search terms or regex pattern.

params.name: mode
params.desc: bm25 (metadata) or regex (against SQL).

params.name: target
params.desc: raw_sql or compiled_sql when in regex mode.

params.name: scope
params.desc: Optional subgraph/layer filter.

tools.returns: Ranked matches, each with the node, the matched snippet, and its DAG context (upstream/downstream neighbors).

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__changeset
tools.purpose: Epoch-based diff of what changed in the project since the last index flush, optionally annotated with downstream impact.

params.name: since_epoch
params.desc: Epoch marker to diff from; defaults to last flush.

params.name: with_impact
params.desc: Attach downstream impact analysis to each change.

tools.returns: List of added/modified/removed nodes and columns since the epoch, optionally with severity-rated downstream impact.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__warehouse
tools.purpose: Run live READ-ONLY SQL directly against the warehouse (Snowflake/BigQuery/etc.) for data previews without a dbt run.

params.name: sql
params.desc: Read-only SQL statement to execute.

params.name: limit
params.desc: Optional row cap for the preview.

tools.returns: Result rows and column headers from the warehouse; read-only, no DDL/DML.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_show
tools.purpose: Execute SQL with full Jinja resolution ({{ ref() }}, {{ source() }}, macros) against the warehouse and return the preview.

params.name: node_or_sql
params.desc: A model selector or inline SQL containing Jinja to resolve and run.

params.name: limit
params.desc: Optional preview row count.

tools.returns: Compiled SQL plus the resulting preview rows after Jinja/macro expansion.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_build
tools.purpose: Structured dbt build (run + test) as an MCP call; auto-refreshes the index on success and supports deferred state.

params.name: select
params.desc: dbt selector, e.g. int_orders_enriched+ for the node and downstream.

params.name: defer
params.desc: Defer unselected refs to a prior state for cost-efficient dev builds.

params.name: state
params.desc: Path/handle to the deferred manifest (e.g. prod).

params.name: exclude
params.desc: Optional selector to exclude.

tools.returns: Per-node run + test status (success/error/skipped) with row counts; triggers an index refresh on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_run
tools.purpose: Structured dbt run as an MCP call; auto-refreshes the index on success and supports deferred state.

params.name: select
params.desc: Models to run via dbt selector syntax.

params.name: defer
params.desc: Defer unselected refs to prior state.

params.name: state
params.desc: Deferred manifest reference.

params.name: full_refresh
params.desc: Force full refresh of incremental models.

tools.returns: Per-model materialization status and timing; index refresh on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_compile
tools.purpose: Structured dbt compile as an MCP call; resolves Jinja to executable SQL without touching the warehouse.

params.name: select
params.desc: Models to compile via dbt selector syntax.

tools.returns: Compiled SQL per selected node; index refresh on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_test
tools.purpose: Structured dbt test as an MCP call; runs schema.yml and data tests and refreshes the index.

params.name: select
params.desc: Tests/models to test via selector syntax.

params.name: defer
params.desc: Defer unselected refs to prior state.

params.name: state
params.desc: Deferred manifest reference.

tools.returns: Per-test pass/fail with failing row counts; index refresh on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_parse
tools.purpose: Structured dbt parse as an MCP call; rebuilds the manifest and validates project graph integrity.

params.name: (none)
params.desc: No required parameters; parses the whole project.

tools.returns: Parse status with any compilation/graph errors; refreshes the index on success.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__context
tools.purpose: Glossary/fiscal/org business-context search with synonym resolution, mapping jargon to canonical dbt terms and linked models.

params.name: term
params.desc: Business phrase to resolve, e.g. "annual revenue".

params.name: scope
params.desc: Optional domain filter: glossary, fiscal, or org.

tools.returns: Canonical term (e.g. ARR), definition, synonyms, and the dbt models/columns that implement it.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__metrics
tools.purpose: MetricFlow semantic-layer interface: list, describe, run, and dry-run metrics.

params.name: action
params.desc: list, describe, run, or dry-run.

params.name: metric
params.desc: Metric name for describe/run/dry-run.

params.name: group_by
params.desc: Dimensions/time grains to group by on run.

params.name: dry_run
params.desc: Validate and show the generated SQL without executing.

tools.returns: Metric catalog, metric definition, or query results (or generated SQL for dry-run).

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__timings
tools.purpose: Build performance analysis: slowest nodes, critical-path bottlenecks, and OpenTelemetry trace data from real run results.

params.name: view
params.desc: slowest, critical_path, or trace.

params.name: top_n
params.desc: How many nodes to return for slowest view.

params.name: node
params.desc: Optional node to scope trace detail to.

tools.returns: Ranked node timings and/or critical-path nodes with durations, plus OpenTelemetry spans.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__diff
tools.purpose: Compare local vs production state for safe PR validation; requires a dbt Cloud token or deferred state.

params.name: select
params.desc: Nodes to diff via selector syntax.

params.name: target
params.desc: Comparison target, e.g. prod.

params.name: state
params.desc: Deferred state/manifest reference for the comparison.

tools.returns: Row-level and schema-level differences between local and production for the selected nodes.

interaction.scenario: An SE is demoing tool selection: they rapid-fire four unrelated prompts at dbt Wizard and watch it route each to the correct mcp__dbt_index__* tool, ending with a deferred build and live diff.
interaction.userPrompt: What breaks if I drop brand_name from stg_products?

events.body: What breaks if I drop brand_name from stg_products?
This is a column-level blast-radius question, not a text search. I'll scope impact to the stg_products node and the brand_name column rather than grepping for the string.
3 downstream columns affected, 1 critical (fct_orders), 2 tests at risk.
Next prompt is a lineage question. Different tool: lineage with a hop limit, downstream only.
Now show me everything two hops downstream of fct_orders.
2 nodes at hop 1, 1 exposure at hop 2.
What's the grain and PK of int_orders_enriched, and are its tests passing?
Grain: one row per order_id. PK order_id. 7 tests pass, 1 fails (not_null on brand_name, 42 rows).

The failing not_null on brand_name lines up with the blast radius from the first prompt. The SE's last ask is to validate a fix safely, so I'll build the subtree with deferred prod state, then diff local vs prod.
Build int_orders_enriched and downstream with deferred prod state, then diff against prod.

events.code:
PASS int_orders_enriched (12.4s)
PASS fct_orders (8.1s)
PASS mart_revenue_daily (3.2s)
PASS mart_brand_performance (2.9s)
4 of 4 models built OK, deferred refs resolved to prod. Index refreshed.
{"node":"fct_orders","schema_changes":[],"row_changes":{"added":0,"removed":0,"modified":42},"note":"42 modified rows: brand_name now populated where prod had NULL"}

events.body: Routed each prompt to the right tool with no file reads or greps. impact: dropping stg_products.brand_name hits 3 downstream columns, fct_orders critical, 2 tests at risk. lineage: fct_orders feeds mart_revenue_daily and mart_brand_performance, then the Looker exposure. describe: int_orders_enriched is one row per order_id keyed on order_id, with a failing not_null on brand_name (42 rows). The deferred dbt_build of int_orders_enriched+ resolved unselected refs to prod and passed all 4 models, and diff vs prod shows exactly those 42 rows now populated with brand_name and zero schema drift. Safe to PR.

seNotes: Lead the demo with tool selection, not output: the point is the wizard picks impact vs lineage vs search correctly from plain English. "What breaks" routes to impact (severity), "show me downstream" routes to lineage (hops), "where is X referenced" routes to search.

Hammer the file-read contrast. Ask the room how a generic agent answers "what breaks if I drop brand_name" — it greps and returns files containing the string. impact returns severity-rated column-level blast radius from real DAG edges, including tests at risk.

Call out that the dbt CLI tools (dbt_build/run/compile/test/parse) are structured calls, not shell strings, and they auto-refresh the index on success — so describe and impact stay current right after a build with no manual reindex.

Always demo --defer --state on dbt_build/dbt_run: it resolves unselected refs to prod state so dev builds only materialize the selected subtree. This is the cost-efficiency talking point for large warehouses.

diff needs a dbt Cloud token or deferred state to have a prod baseline to compare against. If a prospect has neither configured, show warehouse for a live read-only preview instead, then come back to diff once state is wired up.
